description: community area in Chicago, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Logan Square is one of the 77 community areas of Chicago in Illinois, United States.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

Army Air Corps. He was loading the bombs
description: aerial warfare branch of the United States Army from 1941 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Forces (USAAF or AAF) was the major land-based aerial warfare service component of the United States Army and de facto aerial warfare service branch of the United States during and immediately after World War II (1941–1947).

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: cemetery in St. Louis County, Missouri, USA
description_wiki: Jefferson Barracks National Cemetery is an American military cemetery located in St.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Kentucky (US:  , UK: ), officially the Commonwealth of Kentucky, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: capital and largest city of Mississippi, United States
description_wiki: Jackson is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Mississippi.

description: capital of South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Columbia is the capital city of the U.S. state of South Carolina.

description: U.S. military facility in New Jersey
description_wiki: Camp Kilmer is a former United States Army camp in Central New Jersey that was activated in June 1942 as a staging area and part of an installation of the New York Port of Embarkation.

description: largest city of Morocco
description_wiki: Casablanca (, US also ; Arabic: الدار البيضاء, romanized: ad-Dār al-Bayḍāʾ, lit. 'the White House', IPA: [adˈdaːr ɫbajdˤaːʔ]) is the largest city in Morocco and the country's economic and business centre.

description: major theatre of operations during the Second World War
1940 bomber aircraft family
description_wiki: The North American B-25 Mitchell is an American medium bomber that was introduced in 1941 and named in honor of Brigadier General William "Billy" Mitchell, a pioneer of U.S. military aviation.

description: country in North Africa
description_wiki: Tunisia, officially the Republic of Tunisia, is a country in the Maghreb region of North Africa.

description: island in the Mediterranean, region of Italy
description_wiki: Sicily (Italian and Sicilian: Sicilia), officially the Sicilian Region, is an island in the central Mediterranean Sea and one of the twenty regions of Italy, situated south of the Italian Peninsula in continental Europe.

description: 1943-1944 United States military campaign
capital and largest city of Italy
description_wiki: Rome is the capital city and most populated comune (municipality) of Italy.

description: Italian river
description_wiki: The Arno is a river in the Tuscany region of Italy.

description: mountain range stretching 1200 km from the north to the south of Italy, traversing the entire peninsula, and forming the backbone of the country
description_wiki: The Apennines or Apennine Mountains ( AP-ə-nyne; Italian: Appennini [appenˈniːni]) are a mountain range consisting of parallel smaller chains extending c. 1,200 km (750 mi) the length of peninsular Italy.

description: plain in Northern Italy
description_wiki: The Po Valley, Po Plain, Plain of the Po, or Padan Plain (Italian: Pianura Padana, pronounced [pjaˈnuːra paˈdaːna], or Val Padana) is a major geographical feature of northern Italy.

description: region of France
description_wiki: Southern France, also known as the south of France or colloquially in French as le Midi, is a geographical area consisting of the regions of France that border the Atlantic Ocean south of the Marais Poitevin, Spain, the Mediterranean Sea and Italy.

description: American entertainer (1903–2003)
description_wiki: Lester Townes "Bob" Hope (né Leslie Townes Hope; May 29, 1903 – July 27, 2003) was a British-born American comedian, actor, entertainer and producer with a career that spanned nearly 80 years and achievements in vaudeville, network radio, television, and USO Tours.

description: generic nickname
description_wiki:
Axis Sally was the generic nickname given to women radio personalities who broadcast English-language propaganda on behalf of the European Axis powers during World War II. These included:

Mildred Gillars, a German American who broadcast for Nazi Germany.

description: pope of the Catholic Church from 1939 to 1958
description_wiki: Pope Pius XII (Italian: Pio XII; born Eugenio Maria Giuseppe Giovanni Pacelli; 2 March 1876 – 9 October 1958) was head of the Catholic Church and sovereign of Vatican City from 2 March 1939 until his death on 9 October 1958.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: 1949 original cast recording starring Mary Martin, Ezio Pinza

surface_forms: VFW Post #3579 in Park Ridge, Illinois
aliases: Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States
Veterans of Foreign Wars of the U.S.
description: organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The Veterans of Foreign Wars (VFW), formally the Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States, is an organization of United States war veterans who fought in wars, campaigns, and expeditions on foreign land, waters, or airspace as military service members.

description: city in Cook County, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Park Ridge is a city in Cook County, Illinois, United States, and a northwestern suburb of the Chicago metropolitan area.

description: 1943 battle in Tunisia
description_wiki: The Battle of Kasserine Pass was a series of engagements which took place from 19–24 February 1943 around Kasserine Pass, a 2-mile-wide (3.2 km) gap in the Grand Dorsal chain of the Atlas Mountains in west central Tunisia.

description: Italian city, capital of Sicily
description_wiki: Palermo (Sicilian: Palermu) is a city in northwestern Sicily, southern Italy, located on the eponymous gulf facing the Tyrrhenian Sea.

description: United States Army base in New Jersey
description_wiki: Fort Dix, the common name for the Army Support Activity (ASA) located at Joint Base McGuire–Dix–Lakehurst, is a United States Army post.

description: United States military decoration
description_wiki: The Silver Star Medal (SSM) is the United States Armed Forces' third-highest military decoration for valor in combat.

description: United States military award
description_wiki: The Good Conduct Medal is one of the oldest military awards of the United States Armed Forces.

description: country in Northern Europe
description_wiki: Sweden, formally the Kingdom of Sweden, is a Nordic country located on the Scandinavian Peninsula in Northern Europe.

description: country in Northern Europe and North America
description_wiki: Denmark is a Nordic country in Northern Europe.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: land warfare branch of Italy's military forces
description_wiki: The Italian Army (Italian: Esercito Italiano [EI]) is the land force branch of the Italian Armed Forces.

description: (de facto) national library of the United States of America
description_wiki: The Library of Congress (LC or sometimes LOC) is a research library in Washington, D.C., serving as the library and research service for the United States Congress and the de facto national library of the United States.

military unit
